title: paeonia daurica subsp. macrophylla
intro: Paeonia daurica subsp. macrophylla, also known as the Far Eastern peony, is a plant species within the peony family, characterized by its large and showy flowers. Traditionally, Paeonia daurica subsp. macrophylla has been used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties. The plant's roots and flowers are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing pain, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as aiding in the treatment of digestive issues and menstrual cramps. Paeonia daurica subsp. macrophylla is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
